sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the button layout clearly from the ASCII:
Row 42: WWWWWWBBBWWwwwWWBBBWWwwwWWBBBWWWWWWWW
Row 44: WWWWWWWBWWWWwWWWWBWWWWwWWWWwWWWWWWWWW
Row 45: WWWWWWWBWWWWwWWWWBWWWWwWWWWwWWWWWWWWW
Row 46: WWWWWWWBWWWWwWWWWBWWWWwWWWWwWWWWWWWWW
The buttons are:
Row 42 (horizontal bars):
- Left: cols 20-22 (BBB = black), cols 25-27 (www = gray)
- Middle: cols 30-32 (BBB = black), cols 35-37 (www = gray)
- Right: cols 40-42 (BBB = black), no gray bar (already toggled off from earlier)
Rows 44-46 (vertical bars):
- Left: col 21 (B = black), col 26 (w = gray)
- Middle: col 31 (B = black), col 36 (w = gray)
- Right: no black bar, col 41 (w = gray)
So each button has a black part and a gray part. When I click the gray part, it turns black (toggles off). When I click the black part, it turns gray (toggles on).
Currently:
- Left H: black at 20-22, gray at 25-27 → gray is ON
- Middle H: black at 30-32, gray at 35-37 → gray is ON
- Right H: black at 40-42, no gray → gray is OFF (I toggled it earlier)
- Left V: black at 21, gray at 26 → gray is ON
- Middle V: black at 31, gray at 36 → gray is ON
- Right V: no black, gray at 41 → gray is ON
Wait, I need to reset the right horizontal bar. Let me click the black bar at cols 40-42 to turn it back to gray (ON).
Actually, let me think about what the goal might be. The U-shape container has a specific shape - it's like a cup. The T-shape needs to fall into it. The buttons control the lateral movement of the falling object.
For the T-shape to fit into the U-shape, it needs to:
1. Fall down to the right depth
2. Be at the right horizontal position
The U-shape is at cols 29-34, with walls at 29 and 34. The interior is cols 30-33. The T-shape is 4 columns wide (30-33). So it fits horizontally if it stays at cols 30-33.
The default animation has the object move LEFT at step 5, going from cols 30-33 to cols 26-29. This would make it miss the U-shape.
To make the object fall straight down (no lateral movement), I need to cancel the LEFT movement at step 5. The right horizontal bar being OFF gives LEFT at step 5 (same as default). So maybe I need to turn ON the right horizontal bar AND turn OFF some vertical bar to get a RIGHT movement at step 5 to cancel the LEFT.
Wait, but I don't know what the left and right vertical bars do. Let me test those.
Actually, let me first reset all buttons to the default state and then systematically figure out what each button does.
Let me first reset the right horizontal bar by clicking the black bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Reset right horizontal bar by clicking the black bar at cols 40-42
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 42, 'col': 41}])

# Verify all buttons are now in default state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
gray_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w']
black_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] <= 10]
print("Gray bars (ON):")
for n in gray_nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    if b[0][0] == b[1][0]:
        print(f"  H at row {b[0][0]}, cols {b[0][1]}-{b[1][1]}")
    else:
        print(f"  V at col {b[0][1]}, rows {b[0][0]}-{b[1][0]}")
print("Black bars (OFF):")
for n in black_nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    if b[0][0] == b[1][0]:
        print(f"  H at row {b[0][0]}, cols {b[0][1]}-{b[1][1]}")
    else:
        print(f"  V at col {b[0][1]}, rows {b[0][0]}-{b[1][0]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

  V at col 41, rows 44-46
